compatibility_risk.notes: No MCP tool names, Kubernetes resources, .scion-ops paths, branch conventions, template names, kind cluster names, or Scion primitive identifiers are renamed. The spec explicitly prohibits broad search-and-replace and requires evaluation of each string by its role. Existing MCP clients, kind workflows, and session state require no migration.

recommendations: During implementation, ensure snapshot/fixture tests cover both the positive (Holly Ops Drive visible) and negative (MCP tool names, .scion-ops paths, Kubernetes resource names unchanged) cases as specified in task 1.6.
If any future change proposes renaming .scion-ops paths, MCP tool names, or Kubernetes resources, require a separate explicit migration plan per the out-of-scope boundary defined here.

scope_assessment.notes: The change is strictly limited to display-layer branding: web UI title/chrome, app identity copy, coordinator persona display name. All runtime and compatibility surfaces are explicitly called out as out of scope with clear rationale.

spec_findings.notes: Clear in-scope/out-of-scope delineation. Success criteria are concrete and verifiable.

Naming model is unambiguous. Compatibility boundaries section correctly separates display copy from runtime identifiers. Verification strategy includes both positive (branding applied) and negative (identifiers unchanged) checks.
Task 1.5 explicitly guards compatibility surfaces. Task 1.6 requires focused snapshot/fixture tests. Tasks are sequenced appropriately: inventory before implementation, tests before validation.

MODIFIED requirements correctly narrow the existing Operator Overview and Round Detail Timeline requirements to require Holly Ops Drive identity and Holly coordinator display. ADDED requirements for Holly Ops Display Identity and Holly Coordinator Display are well-formed BDD scenarios. All scenarios include explicit guards against relabeling Scion runtime primitives.

artifact_json.summary: The Holly Ops branding OpenSpec is coherent, well-scoped, and preserves all compatibility boundaries. The spec correctly limits the rename to user-facing display copy only, leaving MCP tool names, Kubernetes resource names, .scion-ops session paths, branch names, environment variables, and all Scion runtime primitive terminology unchanged.
